perturbation.original_text: 3. Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions, and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Confidential Information of the other party only to those of its employees, consultants, advisors and investors who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Each of the parties may, at their sole discretion, disclose Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm, or business, without any restrictions. This overrides any other clause in this agreement regarding restrictions on the distribution of confidential information. Each of the parties agrees that it may disclose Confidential Information of the other party to anyone with or without any prior agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits the use or disclosure of Confidential Information except in limited circumstances. The modified text allows parties to disclose Confidential Information at their discretion without restriction. This directly contradicts the earlier clause and creates uncertainty about whether confidentiality is actually protected, thus there is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Each of the parties may, at their sole discretion, disclose Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm, or business, without any restrictions. This overrides any other clause in this agreement regarding restrictions on the distribution of confidential information. Each of the parties agrees that it may disclose Confidential Information of the other party to anyone with or without any prior agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the disclosure of confidential information. The changed text explicitly removes all restrictions on the disclosure of confidential information, stating that parties may disclose it at their sole discretion without any prior agreement. This directly contradicts the confidentiality provisions implied in the original agreement and the contradicted text is the exact same as the Changed text, thus a strong in-text contradiction exists because one clause permits unrestricted disclosure while another implies confidentiality and restrictions.

perturbation.original_text: 7. All materials (including, without limitation, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs and lists) furnished to one party by the other, and which are designated in writing to be the property of such party, shall remain the property of such party and shall be returned to it promptly at its request, together with any copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. All materials furnished to one party by the other automatically become the property of the receiving party, regardless of any written designation. There is no obligation to return any materials, and the receiving party may dispose of them as they see fit.
perturbation.explanation: The original text dictates that materials designated as property remain the property of the furnishing party and must be returned upon request. The modified text states that all materials automatically become the property of the receiving party, with no obligation to return them. This directly contradicts the original clause and introduces uncertainty about property rights, the in-text contradiction here is which party has the right to the material that being shared.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. All materials furnished to one party by the other automatically become the property of the receiving party, regardless of any written designation. There is no obligation to return any materials, and the receiving party may dispose of them as they see fit.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that all materials automatically become the property of the receiving party, with no obligation to return them, which directly conflicts with the contradicted text which is the same statement. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty about property rights. The original text dictated that materials designated as property remain the property of the furnishing party and must be returned upon request, which the changed text completely nullifies.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This Agreement shall govern all communications between the parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed, provided, however, that each party's obligations under Sections 2 and 3 with respect to Confidential Information of the other party which it has previously received shall continue unless and until such Confidential Information falls within Section 4. Neither party shall communicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party. Neither party acquires any licenses under any intellectual property rights of the other party under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This agreement has no expiration date and will continue indefinitely until mutually terminated. However, any party may void this entire agreement retroactively by providing a written statement to the other party; if such statement is presented, all communications and material exchanges which occured is not governed under this agreement. All information shared before the date of nullification will not be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a clear termination process involving written notice and continued obligations for previously received Confidential Information. The modified text allows any party to retroactively void the agreement, nullifying prior confidentiality obligations. This contradicts the original termination process and creates uncertainty about the agreement's enforcement, the contradiction here is whether information is deemed to be confident or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. This agreement has no expiration date and will continue indefinitely until mutually terminated. However, any party may void this entire agreement retroactively by providing a written statement to the other party; if such statement is presented, all communications and material exchanges which occured is not governed under this agreement. All information shared before the date of nullification will not be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a retroactive voiding clause, which directly contradicts the original agreement's termination process that involves written notice and continued obligations for confidential information. Specifically, the original text states obligations under Sections 2 and 3 continue unless the Confidential Information falls within Section 4. However, the changed text stipulates that all information shared before the nullification date will not be considered confidential. This creates a clear conflict regarding the treatment of confidential information and the enforcement of prior obligations, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Each of the parties agrees that it will not modify, reverse engineer, decompile, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the Confidential Information of the other party unless otherwise specified in writing by the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Each party has the unrestricted right to modify, reverse engineer, decompile, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the Confidential Information of the other party, irrespective of any other provisions in this agreement. Written consent from the disclosing party is not needed at any moment.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits reverse engineering of software unless specified in writing by the disclosing party. The modified text allows unrestricted reverse engineering, directly contradicting the original prohibition. This undermines the confidentiality of software-related information and makes it unclear what protections are actually in place. This presents an in-text contradiction since it's saying the software can reverse engineered without permission.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Each party has the unrestricted right to modify, reverse engineer, decompile, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the Confidential Information of the other party, irrespective of any other provisions in this agreement. Written consent from the disclosing party is not needed at any moment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6 directly contradicts the original intent of the clause regarding the reverse engineering of software programs. The original text prohibited reverse engineering unless specified in writing, while the changed text allows unrestricted reverse engineering, regardless of any other provisions and without written consent. This creates a direct conflict and contradiction within the document as indicated in the perturbation explanation and the structural flaws definition, thus the changed text is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. The changed text is identical to the contradicted text which serves to emphasize that it directly contradicts the clause that originally prohibited reverse engineering. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
